raw_consumer_statement:
In XXXX, I used Visa Gap Credit Card managed by XXXX  to purchased international airline tickets for my parents to come visit from XXXX. Flights were canceled due to travel restrictions, then the war started... 

I kept in contact with the XXXX XXXX in order to extend my travel certificate and on XX/XX/XXXX they unexpectedly issued a full refund " to the original form of payment ''. However, the Visa GAP credit card does not exist anymore. 

In XXXX, Gap Credit Card became Master Card and all accounts were moved to Barclays. XXXX assured me that the bank that issued the card ( XXXX  ) would send me a check even though I no longer have an account there. I called XXXX, they said that there is no record of any refunds and if there are any, it would be handled by Barclays. I called Barclays and they said that they have no records of any transactions prior to XXXX and no refunds were posted. 

I had an extensive communication with XXXX XXXX XXXX ( including through XXXX  ). They've conducted a thorough investigation and assured me that the amount was successfully refunded back to the original form of payment. Its been almost three month now since the refund was issued. I spent many days on the phone with both banks and XXXX trying to locate my money and now I am hoping for your help. I have receipts for two refunds ( one per each ticket ) - {$620.00} each or {$1200.00} total, but I have not received that money and I don't know who is responsible - XXXX  or Barclays.
